name: trilkeep
description: Mirror your VS Code notes workspace into TriliumNext via the ETAPI.

trilkeep.connectionName.description: Stable name identifying this Trilium instance (e.g. "real", "test"). The ETAPI token and backup state (manifest) are keyed by this name, so serverUrl can change (e.g. a LAN IP) without losing the token or duplicating the backup. Use distinct names for distinct instances; leave as "default" for a single instance.

trilkeep.serverUrl.description: Base URL of your TriliumNext server (the ETAPI lives at <serverUrl>/etapi). Just the address — change it freely; identity is trilkeep.connectionName.

trilkeep.include.description: Glob patterns for files to back up (relative to the workspace root).

trilkeep.exclude.description: Glob patterns to exclude from backup.

trilkeep.backupOnSave.description: Run an incremental backup automatically whenever a file is saved.

trilkeep.rootNoteTitle.description: Title of this workspace's root note in Trilium. Leave blank to use the workspace folder name. (Branding/grouping lives in trilkeep.group, not here.)

trilkeep.group.description: Slash-path of container notes to nest this workspace's backup root under, e.g. "Trilkeep" or "Trilkeep/work/repo". Trilkeep creates and reuses the containers, so repos sharing a group land together. Leave blank to place the root directly under trilkeep.parentNoteId (or Trilium's root) — i.e. no grouping.

trilkeep.parentNoteId.description: Existing Trilium noteId to use as the base parent instead of Trilium's root (e.g. to nest backups under one of your own notes). The trilkeep.group path, if any, is created under it. Blank = Trilium root.

trilkeep.readOnly.description: Mark the mirrored tree read-only in Trilium's UI (an inheritable #readOnly label on the backup root), to discourage edits in Trilium that the next backup would silently overwrite. On by default because Trilkeep is a one-way mirror (the workspace is the source of truth). Soft UI guard only — Trilkeep's own backups still update the notes. Set false if you want the tree editable in Trilium.

trilkeep.hardDeleteRemovedFiles.description: If true, delete the Trilium note when a backed-up file is removed. Default false (soft): removed files are only logged, never auto-deleted.
